title: SQL injection in n8n Snowflake (and legacy MySQL v1) nodes via unescaped identifiers
description: The Snowflake node and the legacy MySQL v1 node build INSERT and UPDATE statements by directly interpolating user-controlled identifier fields (table name, column names, update key) into the SQL string with no identifier escaping. While row values are bound as parameters, the identifiers are not, so a workflow that maps external user input via expressions into the table/columns/updateKey parameters allows an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL into the constructed query. This is a follow-up to GHSA-f3f2-mcxc-pwjx, whose original fix did not cover the Snowflake node or MySQL v1.
